body:
May 31, 2024

CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

ALL COUNTY LETTER NO. 24-36

The purpose of this All County Letter is to inform County Welfare Departments of the 2.88 percent increase to the vehicle value limit for the California Work Opportunity and Responsibility to Kids, Refugee Cash Assistance, Entrant Cash Assistance and Trafficking and Crime Victims Assistance Programs, effective July 1, 2024.
heading: Page 1

body: May 31, 2024 ALL COUNTY LETTER NO. 24-36 TO: ALL COUNTY WELFARE DIRECTORS ALL COUNTY CALWORKS PROGRAM SPECIALISTS ALL CALFRESH PROGRAM SPECIALISTS ALL COUNTY REFUGEE COORDINATORS ALL COUNTY CONSORTIA REPRESENTATIVES SUBJECT: VEHICLE VALUE LIMIT INCREASE FOR CALIFORNIA WORK OPPORTUNITY AND RESPONSIBILITY TO KIDS, ENTRANT CASH ASSISTANCE, AND TRAFFICKING AND CRIME VICTIMS ASSISTANCE PROGRAM REFERENCE: SENATE BILL (SB) 80 (CHAPTER 440, STATUTES OF 2019); WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE (W&IC) SECTION 11155 AND W&IC SECTION 11257; MANUAL POLICIES AND PROCEDURES (MPP) SECTION 42-207, MPP SECTION 42- 215. , 4 MPP SECTION 69-201.4, MPP SECTION 69-301 AND MPP SECTION 70-105; ALL COUNTY LETTER (ACL) NO. 13-111, ACL NO. 18-76, ACL NO. 19-67 AND ACL NO. 23-47 The purpose of this letter is to inform County Welfare Departments of the 2.88 percent increase to the vehicle value limit for the California Work Opportunity and Responsibility to Kids (CalWORKs), Refugee Cash Assistance (RCA), Entrant Cash Assistance (ECA), and Trafficking and Crime and Victims Assistance Programs (TCVAP), effective July 1, 2024. The W&IC Sections 11155 and 11257 require adjustment of the maximum vehicle limit in accordance with the United States (U.S.) Transportation Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U), as published by the U.S. Department of Labor, Bureau of Labor Statistics.
heading: Page 2

body: All County Letter No. 24-36 Page Two VEHICLE’S VALUE INCREASE Effective July 1, 2024, the maximum value allowed for non-exempt vehicles will increase by 2.88 percent, per the increase reflected in the CPI-U. The new maximum non-exempt vehicle value limit will be $32,968. The equity value for each motor vehicle in excess of $32,968 will be counted towards the maximum asset limit for CalWORKs, for applicants and recipients. Future increases to the maximum CalWORKs non-exempt vehicle value limit will occur annually on July 1, if there is an increase to the CPI-U. REFUGEE CASH ASSISTANCE/ ENTRANT CASH ASSISTANCE AND TRAFFICKING AND CRIME VICTIMS ASSISTANCE PROGRAM The RCA, ECA, and TCVAP cash assistance programs follow the CalWORKs administrative rules with certain exceptions. Pursuant to MPP Section 69-201.4, CalWORKs regulations apply to financial eligibility and payments for RCA, unless specifically superseded by RCA regulations. Unless otherwise provided, ECA and TCVAP recipients must be provided cash assistance under the same conditions and to the same extent as the RCA program per MPP Sections 69-301 and 70- 105, respectively. These implementation instructions for the vehicle maximum allowed non-exempt value increase must also apply to RCA, ECA, and TCVAP applicants and recipients. AUTOMATION Effective July 1, 2024, the Statewide Automated Welfare System (SAWS) will perform the necessary automation to increase the vehicle value limit based on the CPI-U increase. CONTACTS If you have any questions or need additional guidance regarding the information in this letter, contact the Early Engagement and Eligibility Bureau at CWEligibilityPolicy@dss.ca.gov. For RCA, ECA, and TCVAP programs, contact the Refugee Programs Bureau at RefugeePolicy@dss.ca.gov. Sincerely, Original Document Signed By: ALEXIS FERNÁNDEZ GARCIA Deputy Director Family Engagement & Empowerment Division
heading: Page 3

body:
August 2, 2024

CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

ALL COUNTY LETTER NO. 24-54

The purpose of this All County Letter is to inform County Welfare Departments of the 4.32 percent increase to the maximum resource limit for the California Work Opportunity and Responsibility to Kids, Refugee Cash Assistance, Entrant Cash Assistance and Trafficking and Crime Victims Assistance Programs, effective January 1, 2025.
heading: Page 1

body:
August 2, 2024

ALL COUNTY LETTER (ACL) NO. 24-54

TO: ALL COUNTY WELFARE DIRECTORS ALL COUNTY CALWORKS PROGRAM SPECIALISTS ALL CALFRESH PROGRAM SPECIALISTS ALL COUNTY REFUGEE COORDINATORS ALL COUNTY CONSORTIA REPRESENTATIVES

SUBJECT: CHANGES TO MAXIMUM RESOURCE LIMIT FOR FAMILIES:

CALIFORNIA WORK OPPORTUNITY AND RESPONSIBILITY TO KIDS, REFUGEE CASH ASSISTANCE, ENTRANT CASH ASSISTANCE, AND TRAFFICKING AND CRIME VICTIMS ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS

REFERENCE: WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE (WIC) SECTION 11155 AND WIC SECTION 11257; MANUAL OF POLICIES AND PROCEDURES (MPP) SECTION 42-207, MPP SECTION 42-213, MPP SECTION 69- 201.4, MPP SECTION 69-301, AND MPP SECTION 70-105; ALL COUNTY LETTER (ACL) NO. 21-56 AND ACL NO. 22-66

The purpose of this letter is to inform County Welfare Departments (CWDs) of the 4.32 percent increase to the maximum resource limit for the California Work Opportunity and Responsibility to Kids (CalWORKs), Refugee Cash Assistance (RCA), Entrant Cash Assistance (ECA), and Trafficking and Crime Victims Assistance Programs (TCVAP), effective January 1, 2025. The Welfare and Institutions Code (WIC) Sections 11155 and 11257 require CalWORKs to adjust the maximum resource limit in accordance with the California Necessities Index (CNI) each fiscal year.

MAXIMUM ASSET LIMIT INCREASE

Effective January 1, 2025, the maximum resource limit will increase by 4.32 percent, based on the CNI for the most recent fiscal year. The new maximum resource limit for CalWORKs applicants and recipients will be $12,137 or $18,206 for Assistance Units (AUs) that include at least one member who is age 60 or older or disabled.
heading: Page 2

body: All County Letter No. 24-54 Page Two CALWORKS RESTRICTED ACCOUNTS Rules regarding restricted accounts remain unchanged. For guidance on restricted accounts, please see MPP Section 42-213.2. REFUGEE CASH ASSISTANCE (RCA) / ENTRANT CASH ASSISTANCE (ECA) AND TRAFFICKING AND CRIME VICTIMS ASSISTANCE PROGRAM (TCVAP) The instructions provided in this letter also apply to RCA/ECA and TCVAP applicants and recipients. The RCA, ECA, and TCVAP cash assistance programs follow CalWORKs administrative rules with limited exceptions. Pursuant to MPP Section 69-201.4, CalWORKs regulations apply to financial eligibility and payments for the RCA program, unless specifically superseded by RCA regulations. Unless otherwise provided, ECA and TCVAP recipients must be provided cash assistance under the same conditions and to the same extent as the RCA program per MPP Sections 69-301 and 70-105, respectively. NOTICE OF ACTION (NOA) MESSAGE M44-316C (08/22) – No change/mid-period report of property CWDs are reminded to utilize NOA Message M44-316C to notify CalWORKs AUs of the resource limits and to inform AUs that information reported about resources during the certification period has resulted in no change to their benefit amount. The NOA message was revised in 2022 to allow counties to update the resource limits without having to create a new NOA message with every increase. AUTOMATION Effective January 1, 2025, the maximum resource limit will increase. The Statewide Automated Welfare System (SAWS) will perform the necessary automation. CONTACTS If you have any questions or need additional guidance regarding the information in this letter, contact the Early Engagement and Eligibility Bureau at CWEligibilityPolicy@dss.ca.gov For RCA, ECA, and TCVAP programs, contact the Refugee Programs Bureau at RefugeePolicy@dss.ca.gov. Sincerely, Original Document Signed By: ALEXIS FERNÁNDEZ GARCIA Deputy Director Family Engagement and Empowerment Division
heading: Page 3

body:
August 8, 2024

CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY ALL COUNTY LETTER NO. 24-55

This All County Letter informs County Welfare Departments of the California Work Opportunity and Responsibility to Kids Maximum Aid Payment increase pursuant to the passage of Assembly Bill 161 (Chapter 46, Statutes of 2024). This letter includes instructions for implementation of the Maximum Aid Payment increase effective October 1, 2024.
heading: Page 1

body:
August 8, 2024

ALL COUNTY LETTER NO. 24-55

TO: ALL COUNTY WELFARE DIRECTORS ALL COUNTY CALWORKS PROGRAM SPECIALISTS ALL CALFRESH PROGRAM SPECIALISTS ALL COUNTY REFUGEE COORDINATORS ALL COUNTY CONSORTIA REPRESENTATIVES

SUBJECT: MAXIMUM AID PAYMENT (MAP) LEVEL INCREASE FOR CALIFORNIA WORK OPPORTUNITY AND RESPONSIBILITY TO KIDS (CALWORKS)

REFERENCE: WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE (WIC) SECTIONS 11450, WIC 11450.022, WIC 11450.025, WIC 11452, AND WIC 11453; ASSEMBLY BILL (AB) 161 (CHAPTER 46, STATUES OF 2024), AB 85 (CHAPTER 24, STATUTES OF 2013) AND SENATE BILL (SB) 187 (CHAPTER 50, STATUTES OF 2022); MANUAL POLICIES AND PROCEDURES (MPP) SECTION 82-507 AND MPP SECTION 21-115; ALL COUNTY LETTER (ACL) 19-45, ACL 21-101, AND ACL 23-74

The purpose of this All County Letter (ACL) is to inform County Welfare Departments (CWD) of a 0.3 percent increase to the Maximum Aid Payment (MAP) levels pursuant to the passage of Assembly Bill (AB) 161 (Chapter 46, Statutes of 2024), signed by the Governor on July 2, 2024. The CalWORKs MAP increase is effective October 1, 2024.

This letter includes information regarding the effect the MAP increase may have on participants in the Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option. This policy change must be automated into the California Statewide Automated Welfare System (CalSAWS) by the effective date to ensure recipients receive the correct amount of assistance to which they are entitled. If the MAP increases and changes to the Child MAP figures cannot be automated by the effective date, CalSAWS must provide CWDs with a temporary manual process to ensure that CalWORKs recipients receive the MAP
heading: Page 2

body:
All County Letter No. 24-55 Page Two

increase no later than their date of issuance for the month of October. In addition, the CWDs must be provided with a temporary manual process to correctly determine eligibility for the Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option until automation is in place.

CalFresh recipients must receive notification of any negative changes to their households’ CalFresh benefits as a result of the CalWORKs MAP increase no later than September 20, 2024, to meet (10-day) timely noticing requirements.

0.3 PERCENT MAP INCREASE

Assembly Bill (AB) 85 (Chapter 24, Statutes of 2013) redistributed funds to CWDs for CalWORKs expenditures and established the Child Poverty and Family Supplemental Support (CPFSS) Subaccount within the Sales Tax Account of the Local Revenue Fund to support CalWORKs grant increases. The cost of the 0.3 percent grant increase will be funded with revenues deposited into the CPFSS Subaccount. Any remaining amounts in the subaccount will be carried over to future fiscal years. The growth revenues deposited into the CPFSS Subaccount will be monitored to determine if funding remains sufficient for the following fiscal year and thereafter. Each year the subaccount will also be monitored to determine if funds are sufficient to allow an additional increase to the MAP. If funds are sufficient, the increase will take effect on October 1st of that year. If funds are not sufficient to support an increase, no change to the MAP will occur. The California Department of Social Services (CDSS) will notify CWDs in advance of any future grant increases. COUNTY-INITIATED MID-PERIOD ACTION

The grant increase applies to all CalWORKs cases, regardless of whether the Assistance Unit (AU) lives in Region 1 or Region 2 and regardless of the AU’s Exempt or Non-Exempt status. The CWD must treat the grant increase as a mandatory county- initiated mid-period action for Semi Annual Reporting (SAR) and Annual Reporting/Child Only (AR/CO) cases.

INFORMING CALWORKS RECIPIENTS

The CDSS has provided a link to the mass mailer notice TEMP 2250 for the CWD to send to all CalWORKs recipients, informing them of the increase in MAP levels effective October 1, 2024, and the potential decrease to the household’s CalFresh benefits because of the increase to their income. The CWD must begin sending the mailer to all CalWORKs recipients as soon as administratively possible but no later than September 1, 2024. The CDSS strongly encourages the CWD to provide a copy of the TEMP 2250 to all new CalWORKs applicants who apply for aid starting in August 2024 through October 2024, to ensure that they are informed of the October 1, 2024, grant increase.
heading: Page 3

body:
All County Letter No. 24-55 Page Three

The CWD must also send adequate notice of action (NOA) to each household. The CWD must use the TM44-315O to provide CalWORKs recipients information specific to their family’s cash aid amount.

CalFresh noticing rules have not changed. The AUs with CalFresh benefits affected by the grant increase will receive a separate notice about their CalFresh benefits. As a reminder, ACL 21-101 provides guidance on mid-period actions for CalFresh. Information regarding CalWORKs grant changes is considered Verified Upon Receipt (VUR) for CalFresh purposes.

UPDATED CHILD MAP CHARTS

The Child MAP charts are used to determine eligibility for participation in the Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option. The CDSS developed the Child MAP charts to show the Child MAP by region, AU size, and exempt/non-exempt AUs for single child and multi-child participation. The CWD must use the figures in the Child MAP charts to determine if child support received on behalf of a child is greater than the amount of aid the child is eligible for.

The Child MAP charts are updated when there is either an increase in the child support disregard or an increase in the CalWORKs MAP. The attached Child MAP charts have been updated to reflect the statewide MAP increase effective October 1, 2024. The CWD must use the updated figures in the Child MAP charts to determine whether the participating child is still eligible for the option. If the amount of child support received is greater than the figure in the updated charts, then the child is still eligible for the Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option. As stated in MPP Section 82-507.5, if the child support received for the child no longer exceeds the Child MAP figure, the CWD must not add the child back to the AU’s grant, unless it is the SAR 7 or annual redetermination month for the AU. That said, the CWD may add the child back to the grant mid-period at the request of the parent/caretaker relative.

If, at the next SAR or annual redetermination, whichever occurs first, the child support is still below the Child MAP figure, and the parent/caretaker relative has not already made the request, the CWD must add the child back to the AU’s grant.

The CWD must also use the updated Child MAP charts prospectively to determine eligibility when a parent/caretaker relative elects to have a CalWORKs eligible child in their home participate in the Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option.

FORMS AND NOTICES OF ACTION TEMP 2250 (06/24) – State Law Changes the Maximum Aid Payment Levels for Cash Aid Recipients – This form was created for the CWD to send to all CalWORKs AUs to inform them of the change to the MAP. This is a required form with no substitutes permitted.
heading: Page 4

body:
All County Letter No. 24-55 Page Four

TM44-315O (07/24) – NOA message – Grant Increase/Law Change – This NOA message was created to send to cash assistance AUs when their grant increases due to the MAP change. Instructions for use of the NOA message are included on the TM44-315O. This message is required with no substitutes permitted to the language. The CWDs must fill in case-specific information as instructed.

Required Form – No Substitutes Permitted

Both the TEMP 2250 and the TM44-315O are Required Forms – No Substitutes Permitted. Forms in this category are required forms that the CWD may not modify or restructure. However, overprinting or reformatting under the conditions outlined in Operations Manual Section 23-400.211, Overprinting Required Forms, and Section 23- 400.212, Electronic Data Processing (EDP) Modifications, is permitted.

COPIES AND TRANSLATIONS

Forms referenced in this letter are available on the CDSS Forms/Brochures webpage. When CDSS completes all translations of a form, they are posted on the Translated Forms and Publications webpage. When made available by CDSS, forms translated into an individual’s preferred language must be provided to the individual pursuant to Manual of Policies and Procedures (MPP) Section 21-115.2. For questions on translated materials, please contact Language Services at (916) 651-8876. If translations are not available, recipients who have elected to receive materials in languages other than English should be sent the English version of the form or notice along with the GEN 1365-Notice of Language Services and a local contact number.

Per Government Code Section 7290, et seq., the CWDs must ensure that effective bilingual services are provided. This requirement may be met through utilization of paid interpreters, qualified bilingual employees, and qualified employees of other agencies or community resources. These services must be provided, free of charge, to the applicant/participant. If CDSS does not provide translations of a form, it is the county’s responsibility to read and interpret the form if an applicant or recipient requests it.

Additionally, the CWDs must provide auxiliary aids and services to persons who are deaf or hearing impaired, or persons with impaired speech, vision, or manual skills, where applicable. More information regarding provisions for services to applicants and recipients who have limited English proficiency or who have disabilities can be found in MPP Section 21-115 and ACL 19-45.
heading: Page 5

body:
All County Letter No. 24-55 Page Five

CLAIMING INSTRUCTIONS

Impacted claims reflecting the appropriate estimated sharing ratios as a result of this MAP increase will be released in a forthcoming County Fiscal Letter.

If you have any questions regarding this letter, please contact the CalWORKs Early Engagement & Eligibility Bureau at CWEligibilityPolicy@dss.ca.gov.

Sincerely,

Original Document Signed By

ALEXIS FERNÁNDEZ GARCIA Deputy Director Family Engagement and Empowerment Division Attachments cc: County Welfare Directors Association of California (CWDA)

CWDs shall use the figures noted as “Child MAP” to determine if the amount of child support is greater than the amount the child would receive for CalWORKs (Please note for simplicity, income would not be a factor and a straight MAP would be used).

If the amount of child support received is greater than the “Child MAP” for the child’s AU size with the child included, region and AU type, the child is able to participate in Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant if all other conditions are met.

Example 1, the child resides in Region 1. The AU size would be 3 with the child included. It is a non-exempt AU so the base Child MAP for the child is $245.00, adding the $200.00 child support “passthrough” or “disregard”, the total child MAP is $445.00. The child receives $450.00 in child support. Therefore, the child can participate if they meet the other required conditions because they receive more than $445.00 in child support.

Example 2, same scenario as above except the child receives only $350.00 in child support. The child cannot participate because the child support is less than the $445.00 child MAP.

heading: Page 13

body:
Child Maximum Aid Payment (MAP) Chart Effective October 1, 2024

Multiple Children Participating in Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option

Multi-child opt-in to Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option is not available for an AU size of two. At least one CalWORKs aided child must be in the AU

Counties shall use the figures noted as “Child MAP” to determine if the amount of child support is greater than the amount the child would receive for CalWORKs when the parent/caretaker relative has requested to have more than one child participate in Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option (Please note for simplicity the California Department of Social Services made the decision that income would not be a factor and a straight MAP would be used). If the amount of child support received is greater than the “Child MAP” for the child’s AU size with the child included, region and AU type, the child may participate in Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option if all other conditions are met. Counties and the parent/caretaker relative may use the child support received by each child to determine who to assign as child 1, child 2 etc., when the children are opting into Child Support in Lieu of CalWORKs Grant Option at the same time based on the amount of child support it takes to meet the eligibility threshold for the child in the AU size/type/region and assignment of child order (child 1, child 2 etc.).
